sections.paragraph:
Ταρσός, οῦ, ἡ (the sing. form of the name in Diod S 14, 20, 2; Strabo 14, 5, 9; Dio Chrys. 16 [33], 17; 17 [34], 46; Arrian, Anab. 2, 4, 7; Joseph., ins) Tarsus, capital of Cilicia in southeast Asia Minor (Diod S, loc. cit., μεγίστη τῶν ἐν Κιλικίᾳ πόλεων) famous as a seat of Gk. learning Ac 9:30; 11:25; 21:39 D; 22:3 (Τ. τῆς Κιλικίας as Diod S 20, 108, 2; Xenophon Eph. 2, 13, 5; Jos., Ant. 9, 208).—WRamsay, The Cities of St. Paul 1907, 85–244; HBöhlig, Die Geisteskultur v. Tarsos 1913; HSteinmann, Z. Werdegang d. Pls. D. Jugendzeit in Tarsus 1928; WvanUnnik, Tarsus or Jerusalem ’62; AJones, The Cities of the Eastern Roman Provinces2 ’71; Pauly-W. IV 2413–39; Kl. Pauly V 529f; BHHW III 1933; Haenchen on Ac 22:3. S. also CHemer, Acts index.; s. also s.v. Κιλικία.—PECS 883f. Schürer III 33f (lit.).

sing. sing. = singular

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

Gk. Gk. = Greek

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Z Z = Zeitschrift

Pls. Pls. = Paulus (Paul, in German titles)

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

PECS PECS = Princeton Encyclopedia of Classical Sites—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 991
